source_formulae: P0R03319: The top-down signal from the generative model (the prediction) is physically instantiated as the selective pressure exerted by the Psi-field during einselection. The act of "predicting" a state is the act of biasing the quantum collapse towards that state. This is the most fundamental level of top-down causation.

specs.section_path: 15-Layer Summary Table > 4.1 The Cosmic Algorithm: HPC & Active Inference > The Physical Mechanism of Downward Causation: Amplification of Intent > Meta-Framework Integrations
source_formulae: P0R03321: A prediction error is a mismatch between the prediction and the sensory data. In this model, the weak signal from the selected quantum state can be seen as a nascent error signal. QSR is the mechanism that determines if this error signal is "important" enough to be amplified and broadcast across the cortical hierarchy to force a model update. The quasicritical state ensures the system is maximally sensitive to these subtle but important error signals, allowing it to learn and adapt efficiently.
test_protocols: preserve QSR is Error-Signal Amplification: source-accounting boundary
